commit_message: got a physical unit, starting bringup tests

user_prompt: if there are 0 devices, then just idle forever here
choke the FIFOs going to the file because we are not using AudioFileRecorder at all in this test
connect to the first device in the list as a HFP
and just pump audio from Bluetooth callback to the speakers forever
implement the rest of the code, like #includes other scaffolding that I missed
agent_reply: - Initializes Serial, NVS, BluetoothManager, and AudioManager
- Prints local BT address, PIN, BT state, bonded device count/list, and connection status
- Idles forever when no bonded devices exist
- Connects HFP to the first bonded device
- Pumps Bluetooth audio to speaker forever
I also added the missing declaration in [all_tests.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.h).
Note: [all_tests.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.cpp) still calls `test_btpairing()`. To run this new test, switch `run_test()` to call `test_btspeakerphone()`.
- Initializes Serial, NVS, BluetoothManager, and AudioManager
- Prints local BT address, PIN, BT state, bonded device count/list, and connection status
- Idles forever when no bonded devices exist
- Connects HFP to the first bonded device
- Pumps Bluetooth audio to speaker forever
I also added the missing declaration in [all_tests.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.h).
Note: [all_tests.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.cpp) still calls `test_btpairing()`. To run this new test, switch `run_test()` to call `test_btspeakerphone()`.

commit_message: visual indicator for mic recorder test

user_prompt: Initialize serial port for printing, and also AudioManager and AudioFileRecorder, that should also mean MicGainManager is initialized, as that is what this test is about
Make sure the ClockAgent is synced to compiler time
Start a recording but not using the real hardware, instead
synthesize a buffer that is roughly a 440 Hz tone, roughly, it starts and ends at 0 phase
it has a peak-to-peak that is only 10% of full scale and a DC offset of -10% of full scale
we need to generate this every loop
every loop, queue an unmodified copy of this into the bt2file FIFO, queue a MicGainManager processed copy into the mic2file FIFO, then let the AudioFileRecorder write it into the file
do this such that 10 seconds worth of audio is submit

I will feed the result into the decoder, if everything works, I will see one channel with an unmodified tone, and one channel with the filtered tone, with the DC offset disappearing, and the amplitude growing up to 3X the original
I can run this test over and over again with different parameters to see different responses
Warn me if I've made a mistake here
implement what I have written here in the IDE
agent_reply: - Generates 10 seconds of synthetic mono tone at ~440 Hz, with 10% full-scale peak-to-peak and `-10%` DC offset.
- Pumps/stops the recorder and prints the file path plus final stats.
I also added the prototype in [all_tests.h](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.h:17) and selected it in [all_tests.cpp](/c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/all_tests.cpp:13).

Small warning: at 16 kHz, 440 Hz is clean over exactly 10 seconds, but individual 240-sample chunks do not begin/end at zero phase. The generated phase is continuous across chunks, so the recording should still be clean.

commit_message: added a mic preprocessor, HPF and AGC
speakerphone test works, it did involve editing the esp-idf config and building the library in the back
good progress working on GUI but ran into memory problems with PNG decode
progress on bluetooth but cannot initiate connection, can handle incoming though
recording works great now, tweaked the ui

user_prompt: I want to run `void test_sdcard()` to test performance of the recorder with encryption enabled
make sure everything is wired so that it works with encryption actually built and active
agent_reply: Wired `test_sdcard()` so it should actually test encrypted recorder performance now.
- `setup()` now calls `all_init()` before bring-up tests, so NVS/M5/gui basics exist before `Aegis` and SD setup.
- installs a deterministic test master key only if no key already exists,
- forces `AudioFileRecorder::setPurePcmMode(false)` so it does not bypass encryption.
- `AudioFileRecorder` now includes `Aegis` by relative path so PlatformIO library discovery works.
- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.
Wired `test_sdcard()` so it should actually test encrypted recorder performance now.
- `setup()` now calls `all_init()` before bring-up tests, so NVS/M5/gui basics exist before `Aegis` and SD setup.
- installs a deterministic test master key only if no key already exists,
- forces `AudioFileRecorder::setPurePcmMode(false)` so it does not bypass encryption.
- `AudioFileRecorder` now includes `Aegis` by relative path so PlatformIO library discovery works.
- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.
